info.title: Simulator.Company REST API
info.description:
Simulator.Company Public REST API.

# Authentication

<!-- ReDoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of rows to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

get.description:
This API is used to get system forms:
* Graphs
* Layers
* Streams
* Events
* Widgets
* Scripts
* Locations
* Tags
* Reactions

Scopes [control.events:forms.readonly]

parameters.name: isTemplate
parameters.description: Specify true if the form is a template; otherwise, false

post.description: Adds an account-currency pair to the specified form. Scopes [control.events:forms.management]

treeCalculation.description: Enable tree calculation for the account

minLimit.description: Minimum limit for the account balance

maxLimit.description: Maximum limit for the account balance

limitServiceUrl.description: URL of the service used to check account limits

commissionServiceUrl.description: URL of the service used to calculate commissions

userId.description: ID of the user who created the record

delete.summary: Clear cache for form item options
delete.description:
Helps to clear cache for form item options, where source type is 'api'.

Scopes [control.events:forms.management]

parameters.name: edgeTypeId
parameters.description:
Link type ID from the list of available [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes).\
To get only hierarchical links use a link type with the name `hierarchy`,
to get only links for calculating balances - a link type with the name `calculatedTree`.

parameters.name: edgeTypeId
parameters.description:
Link type ID from the list of available [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes).\
To get only hierarchical links use a link type with the name `hierarchy`,
to get only links for calculating balances - a link type with the name `calculatedTree`.

edgeTypeId.description: Link type ID from the list of [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of relation to the actor, `linked` means both child and parent actors

parameters.name: edgeTypeId
parameters.description:
Link type ID from the list of available [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes).\
To get only hierarchical links use a link type with the name `hierarchy`,
to get only links for calculating balances - a link type with the name `calculatedTree`.

parameters.name: formId
parameters.description: Form ID to filter linked actors

parameters.name: exceptFormId
parameters.description: Form ID to exclude actors from the result

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of actors to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

parameters.name: withForm
parameters.description: Specify true to add form info to response data

parameters.name: lastReaction
parameters.description: Specify true to add last actor reaction to response data

parameters.name: reactionsStats
parameters.description: Specify true to add users' reactions statistics to response data

get.description:
Scopes [control.events:actors.readonly]

A layer is an actor created with system form [Layers](api.html#operation/getSystemForms).
This API retrieves all layer actor data together with it's nodes and links.

parameters.name: noDuplicate
parameters.description: Specify false to add all nodes copies on layer

nodes.description: List of actors on the layer

layerStarred.description: Actor is marked with flag on layer

readonly.description: Actor is signed or executed and can't be edited

edges.description: List of links on the layer

laIdSource.description: Source actor ID on layer (for type=edge)

laIdTarget.description: Target actor ID on layer (for type=edge)

height.description: Height of an element on layer

width.description: Width of an element on layer

put.summary: Save layer nodes positions on layer

nodes.description: List of actors on the layer

edges.description: List of links on the layer

pinned.description: A value that indicates whether the layer was pinned on the interface.

laId.description: Unique element ID on source layer

post.description:
Scopes [control.events:actors.readonly]

This method searches an element on the layer and returns a list of their layer-unique IDs.

get.summary: Get layer actors by form ID
get.description:
Scopes [control.events:actors.readonly]

This method returns all layer actors filtered by form ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of actors to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

parameters.name: reactionsCount
parameters.description: Specify true to add actor reactions count to response data

parameters.name: members
parameters.description: Filter by user access rights, separated by commas

get.summary: Get all graph layers with actor
get.description:
Scopes [control.events:actors.readonly]

This method retrieves all layers where the actor is added.

ref.description: A string value for making actors API requests by ref (must be unique within a form). No need to use for flowCharts

cardActorId.description: Actor ID to display its data card

parameters.name: attachments
parameters.description: Flag to add attachments list to response

parameters.name: reactionsStats
parameters.description: Flag to add actor reactions statistics by users to response

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of fields we want to receive for every actor in response data.
To add actor's form fields (`data`), specify every demanded field as `data.item_16357762770841362`.
Don't pass this parameter to get full actors' info.

readonly.description: Actor is signed or executed and can't be edited

reactionsStats.description: Object with actor reactions statistics by users (if `reactionsStats` flag enabled)

<userId>.description: Object with exist reactions numbers for exact user

attachments.description: List of actor's attached files (if `attachments` flag enabled)

parameters.name: replaceEmpty
parameters.description: Specify false to update only those actor fields that are explicitly passed in the request body. Otherwise, non-passed fields will be cleared.

parameters.name: objId
parameters.description: User ID to get system actor

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of actors to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of actors to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.description:
Specify true to retrieve large sets of results.
The response will contain a scroll ID in the response body. 
You can then use the scroll ID to retrieve the next batch of results for the request.

parameters.name: scrollId
parameters.description:
Scroll ID from the previous response to retrieve the next batch of results.
Scroll ID expires in 1 minute.

parameters.name: orderByKey
parameters.description: It`s the key by which the sorting will be performed

parameters.name: incomeType
parameters.description: Use with accountNameId and currencyId, if it is null, filter will be applied to total balance

parameters.name: isSystem
parameters.description: Specify true to filter only system actors, false - only custom actors. Don't pass this parameter to get both types of actors.

parameters.name: q

edgeTypeId.description: Link type ID from the list of [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes)
Link type ID from the list of [link types](api.html#operation/getEdgeTypes)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of account names to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

parameters.name: withStats
parameters.description: We strongly recommend including withStats=false in your requests, otherwise the performance of the method will be significantly reduced

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The total number of currencies to retrieve (used with offset)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to skip when fetching list

get.description:
Scopes [control.events:actors.readonly] Link type is an attribute of every link between actors. E.g., on the Economics layer - a link type with the name `calculatedTree` is used, on other layers - a hierarchical link (with the name `hierarchy`). There is a set of specific link types used for links between actors in the accounts tree. For every existing actor's account (unique pair of name and currency), there is a dedicated link type.
Such link type name is a concatenation of two ID fields: `_` (Learn more about [account names](../#operation/getAccountNames), [currencies](../#operation/getCurrencies))

parameters.name: isSystem
parameters.description: Specify true to filter only system actors

isTree.description: A boolean flag to indicate if link type is used for accounts tree

get.description:
The scope depends on what object do you want to manage access to.

| Object type | Scope |
| ------- | --------|
| formTemplate | [control.events:forms.readonly] |
| actor | [control.events:actors.readonly] |

parameters.name: objId
parameters.description: Object ID to get access info. For account use compound ID `nameId_currencyId`.

post.description:
The scope depends on what object do you want to manage access to.

| Object type | Scope |
| ------- | --------|
| formTemplate | [control.events:forms.management] |
| actor | [control.events:actors.management] |

parameters.name: filterIncorrect
parameters.description: If you pass true, the request will be successfully executed even if the user id in the user array contains a user id that is not in the workspace.

parameters.name: objId
parameters.description: Object ID to share. For account use compound ID `nameId_currencyId`.

OAuth2.description:
Control Events use the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization. Control Events supports common OAuth 2.0 scenarios such as those for web server, installed, and client-side applications.

If you want to make requests on behalf of other control.events users, you can register an OAuth application and have them grant permissions to your app.

BearerAuth.description:
You can generate an API access_token for your workspace on the Account (Account -> API Keys).

To use an API access_token to authenticate an API request, just replace access_token in the following API endpoints with the string key, and pass the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN
